text: The surgeon positions the robotic ports in a low transverse arrangement, with one port located in the suprapubic area, preparing to upsize this port for the extraction incision.
The surgeon positions the robotic ports in a low transverse arrangement, with arm number two located in a suprapubic position, preparing to upsize this port for the extraction incision.
The surgeon uses a twelve millimeter port to resect the mesentery, carefully ensuring grossly negative margins and removing a rim of mesenteric tissue.
The surgeon uses a twelve millimeter port to resect the mesentery, ensuring grossly negative margins by taking a rim of the mesentery tissue.
In firefly mode, the surgeon uses a stapler to divide the margins of the small bowel, with enhanced visualization of the tissue through fluorescence imaging.
In firefly mode, the surgeon introduces a stapler to divide the margins of the tissue, with the fluorescent imaging highlighting the targeted areas for resection.

The surgeon aligns the antiparastaltic side-to-side anastomosis, creating enterotomies in the bowel. A stay suture is placed on the near side to assist in positioning the bowel onto the vascular load stapler, which is introduced through the suprapubic port.

The surgeon aligns the antiparastaltic side-to-side anastomosis, creating enterotomies in the bowel. A stay suture is placed on the near side to assist in pulling the bowel onto the vascular load stapler, which is introduced through the suprapubic port.
The surgeon removes the stay suture and begins to close the common channel using a 3-0 V-Loc suture, starting at the near corner and performing an over-and-over stitch technique.

The surgeon cuts the stay suture and begins to close the common channel using a 3-0 V-Loc suture. Starting at the near corner, the surgeon performs an over-and-over stitch, then imbricates the tissue while sewing back down with the same suture.
The surgeon uses the same suture to imbricate the tissue, folding and securing it as they move back down along the previously sutured area.
The surgeon performs an over-and-over stitch technique, progressing towards the far corner of the surgical site.
The surgeon continues the over-and-over stitching technique, progressing towards the far corner of the anastomosis.
The surgeon begins to imbricate the tissue, using a continuous suture technique to fold and secure the layers back down.

The surgeon begins to imbricate the bowel, ensuring a single-layer closure of the enterotomy with a canal suture technique to avoid exposed puckering mucosa. The suture covers the near corner thoroughly, creating a two-layer closure throughout.
The surgeon ties a knot to secure the anastomosis, covering it with omentum and burying it beneath the transverse colon. The specimen is extracted through the suprapubic port site.
The surgeon covers the anastomosis with omentum, burying it beneath the transverse colon. The specimen is extracted through the suprapubic port site.
